spec.goal: Make the review-request entrypoint explicit: the initial PR submission relies on automatic Codex review request behavior, while later pushed heads require an `@codex review` comment to request another review round.
success_criteria: review-request/SKILL.md states that the repo-approved review entrypoint is automatic on the first PR submission and `@codex review` on later pushed heads
dev/review-request/run_smoke.py guards the explicit automatic-first and `@codex review`-after-push contract text
home/codex/AGENTS.md mirrors the same review-request behavior
the installed runtime review-request skill copy and live ~/.codex/AGENTS.md reflect the updated wording after sync and activation
constraints: Keep the change scoped to explicit review-request entrypoint behavior
Do not rename skills or restructure the lifecycle beyond wording and smoke coverage
Edit only the touched skill, paired smokes, and matching codex AGENTS guidance
Use the existing review lifecycle branch and keep verification focused

tasks.title: Explicitly define the review-request entrypoint
tasks.objective: Update review-request so the initial PR submission is automatic and later pushed heads use an `@codex review` comment, then harden the review-request smoke to assert that exact behavior.
outputs: Smoke assertions for automatic initial review and follow-up `@codex review` comments

tasks.title: Mirror the same entrypoint rule in AGENTS
tasks.objective: Update home/codex/AGENTS.md and its source smoke so the lifecycle policy says initial PR submission is automatic and later pushed heads use `@codex review` via review-request.
outputs: Source smoke assertions for the explicit entrypoint behavior

tasks.title: Sync runtime copies and verify
tasks.objective: Sync the installed review-request skill and live AGENTS surface to the new wording, then rerun the focused verification set.

triggers: The requested behavior requires changing the review lifecycle stage boundaries instead of wording and smoke updates
The repo-approved review entrypoint differs across repos and cannot be expressed as one shared codex policy line
Runtime sync requires a broader installer change rather than touched-surface updates

evidence: 2026-03-19: Local probe showed that review-request still says only 'current repo-approved review entrypoint' and does not encode the explicit automatic-first versus `@codex review`-after-push behavior.

2026-03-19: Updated review-request/SKILL.md and dev/review-request/run_smoke.py so the initial PR submission path is automatic and later pushed heads use an `@codex review` comment; verified with python3 dev/review-request/run_smoke.py and git diff --check in the skills workspace.

2026-03-19: Updated /Users/xavier/Documents/nix/home/codex/AGENTS.md and dev/smoke/codex/run_scout_skeptic_source_smoke.py to mirror the same review-request entrypoint rule; verified with python3 dev/smoke/codex/run_scout_skeptic_source_smoke.py and git diff --check in the nix repo.

2026-03-19: Synced /Users/xavier/.codex/skills/review-request/SKILL.md to the updated source wording, ran sudo -n -H darwin-rebuild switch --flake /Users/xavier/Documents/nix#acg-box, and verified the live /Users/xavier/.codex/AGENTS.md surface now contains the automatic-first and `@codex review`-after-push rule.

local_checkpoint.current_theory: The contract gap is in review-request wording, not in review-repair or delivery-prepare.
local_checkpoint.contradictory_evidence: If some repo-specific policy already hardcoded `@codex review` everywhere, adding automatic-first behavior here would conflict with existing repo-approved entrypoints.
local_checkpoint.missing_evidence: Need to patch review-request, AGENTS, and both smokes, then verify the runtime copies match.
local_checkpoint.next_action: Update review-request and its smoke first, then mirror the same explicit entrypoint rule in codex AGENTS and runtime surfaces.
